key_metric: PEG 0.42 (fwd P/E 18.5x vs 85.4% earnings growth) but FCF yield only 1.59% on $1.7T cap
one_line: Genuinely high-quality AI-infra compounder (76% gross / 49% op margin, 37% ROE, $100B+ FY27 contracted) selling at a low PEG only if the forward EPS triple materializes; today's cash generation doesn't yet support the price.
bull: Revenue growth 47.9% and earnings growth 85.4% against a fwd P/E of 18.5x gives a PEG of 0.42 -- statistically cheap for the growth rate. Op margin 49% and gross margin 76.3% are best-in-class, ROE 37.3% confirms high capital efficiency. The trailing-to-forward P/E collapse (59.6x -> 18.5x) is explained by a known, dateable catalyst (VMware amortization rolloff) layered on AI ASIC ramp, not a speculative multiple expansion story. $100B+ FY27 revenue is contracted/design-cycle-locked (3-5yr custom silicon programs with Google TPU, Meta MTIA), giving unusual revenue visibility for a semis name. Analyst sentiment is strongly supportive (recommendation_mean 1.31 across 45 analysts), institutional ownership is high (79.9%) and short interest is negligible (1.32%) -- no crowded-short skepticism embedded in the tape. The -27.5% drawdown from the 52w high is a real repricing, not just noise, and it happened on a 'no guidance raise' disappointment rather than a fundamental miss -- i.e., the business didn't get worse, the market's tolerance for imperfection did.
bear: The entire cheap-PEG thesis rests on the forward estimate, not on trailing, GAAP-confirmed earnings -- trailing P/E of 59.6x is the honest current price of the business today. For the fwd multiple to be real, EPS needs to roughly triple; any slippage in the VMware amortization rolloff timeline or AI ASIC ramp cadence re-rates the stock on a still-expensive trailing basis. FCF yield of just 1.59% on a $1.7T market cap is the tell: at ~$27B of FCF against $1.7T cap, the company is NOT currently cash-cheap -- the market is pricing in FCF that has not shown up yet, which is a forecast bet dressed as a value metric. Customer concentration is real and structural: the $100B+ contracted revenue is locked to a handful of hyperscalers (Google, Meta, reportedly a 3rd), each of which controls its own AI capex cycle and has both the balance sheet and the incentive to eventually insource more silicon design -- these are sophisticated counterparties, not sticky retail-style customers. The post-earnings 12-15% drop on merely 'no raised guidance' (not a cut) shows the stock is priced for perfection with zero margin for a soft quarter. Semis/AI-infra capex is famously cyclical; a hyperscaler capex pause (macro-driven or ROI-driven) hits AVGO's revenue growth and collapses the PEG-cheap narrative fast.
margin_of_safety: Thin and estimate-dependent, not asset- or cash-flow-backed. The -27.5% drawdown provides some cushion and the low PEG (0.42) looks statistically attractive, but it is only a real margin of safety if the ~3x forward EPS step-up delivers on schedule -- there is no hard valuation floor here (FCF yield of 1.59% offers no support, trailing P/E of 59.6x is rich). This is a quality-compounder-on-sale entry for investors willing to underwrite hyperscaler capex durability and 2-3 name customer concentration, not a Graham-style bargain with balance-sheet or cash-flow protection. Sizing should reflect that the risk is squarely on the estimate, not on the operating business.
